entries.notes:
Color differs (MATLAB blue vs Python grey) and Python adds axis
labels with tick values; structure identical (same trajectory,
same spike locations).  Baseline 0.498 measured 2026-06-11.

entries.figure_description: KS plot — linear vs quadratic GLM
entries.notes: After Phase 2 fix, Quadratic uses centered features
Stochastic spike draws differ between numpy and MATLAB random streams
entries.skip_reason: V2 iter 9 removed surplus cells; fig_006 no longer emitted. Re-baseline pending after MATLAB re-extraction.

entries.notes:
After Phase 2 fix (maskAwayAllExcept reset + label parser +
setNeighbors), the estimated panel matches the actual panel's
sign pattern.  Magnitude unstable at l2=0; sign is what matters.
entries.skip_reason: V2 iter 9 removed surplus cells; fig_013 no longer emitted. Re-baseline pending after MATLAB re-extraction.

entries.notes: Stochastic spike draws differ; tracks the same sinusoid
Baseline 0.631 measured 2026-06-11; layout match good.

entries.figure_description: Constant Mg raster + Poisson model fit
entries.notes:
After Phase 2 sampleRate fix (200→1000 Hz), the design-matrix
dimension matches MATLAB so the rate-axis ranges agree.

After Phase 1 + Phase 2 fixes (l2=0.0 + sampleRate=1000 +
epoch boundaries [495,765]), coefficients should match MATLAB
magnitudes within stochastic-data tolerance.

After Phase 2 fix (8 hand-typed → 100 seeded random spikes
matching ``sort(rand(1,100))``), figure scope matches MATLAB.
Stochastic seed differs between numpy and MATLAB streams.

entries.figure_description: Cell 25 Gaussian vs Zernike place field (3D mesh overlay)
entries.skip_reason:
MATLAB renders an overlaid 3D wireframe at specific camera angle;
Python uses plot_wireframe with view_init(elev=30, azim=-37.5).
Layout/perspective mismatch is too large for SSIM until both
paths agree on a single rendering convention.

MATLAB overlays the scaled CIF on the raster with log-y axis;
Python plots a plain raster only.  Layout pending Phase 4.

entries.notes:
Baseline 0.470 measured 2026-06-11; significant layout drift
(MATLAB renders multi-axis SignalObj with custom labels,
Python uses default matplotlib subplots).  Candidate for
Phase 4 pedagogical fix.

Baseline 0.273 measured 2026-06-11; Python raster layout differs
substantially from MATLAB's multi-panel published figure.
Marker: gate baselines current state; lower threshold prevents
false alarms but a future Phase 4 fix should target 0.50+.

Baseline 0.214 measured 2026-06-11; similar to
nSpikeTrainExamples — Python collection raster doesn't reproduce
MATLAB's published multi-panel layout.  Phase 4 candidate.
